page: Vultr vhp-4c-8gb-intel is a High Performance virtual server featuring 4 shared vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon cores with hyper-threading enabled. Operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz, the x86_64 architecture includes 32 KB L1d cache, 4 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache. The system is provisioned with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and includes 180 GB of local NVMe SSD storage alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark evaluations indicate mixed performance characteristics. Single-core stress-ng performance and small-block cached memory read bandwidth of 416.34 GB/sec perform at average levels, while multi-core stress-ng and large-block uncached memory reads of 33.43 GB/sec fall into the weak category. Cryptographic and database operations also register lower-tier results, with a PassMark database score of 1279.7. However, the server demonstrates moderate capability in Redis SET operations, achieving 6.06 million operations per second, and bzip3 single-threaded decompression at 8.95 GB/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation, this instance presents a resource-dense, cost-efficient option for workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated CPU performance. The inclusion of 180 GB NVMe SSD storage provides fast local I/O, offsetting the weaker multi-core processing and memory bandwidth limitations. This configuration is best suited for lightweight web serving, Redis caching, basic file decompression, and development environments rather than intensive database hosting or large-scale multi-threaded computations.
description: Vultr vhp-4c-8gb-intel is a High Performance virtual server configured with 4 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 180 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, this instance features AVX-512 instruction support and includes one public IPv4 address. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities and Redis throughput of 6.06 million operations per second, though multi-core processing and uncached memory bandwidth are relatively weak. This resource profile offers a cost-effective solution for workloads with intermittent CPU demands. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, caching layers, and basic development tasks.
og_description: Vultr vhp-4c-8gb-intel is a High Performance virtual server with 4 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and 180 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering balanced single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-4c-8gb-intel features 4 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and 180 GB NVMe SSD storage for cost-effective, lightweight workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server combining shared Intel Xeon processing with fast local NVMe storage for lightweight caching and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: 4 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory
180 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Average single-core performance and 6.06 million Redis SET operations per second
Supports AVX-512 and AES hardware-accelerated instructions
Best suited for lightweight web serving, caching, and development environments
